programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs goes into his favourite diner for a cup of coffee and finds himself staring down the barrel of a gun, forcing him to question choices he has made in the past and present. The action then flashes back a few days to when he and the rest of the team members were investigating the death of a petty officer on board USS Gentry, which has been undergoing repairs in dry dock. The 200th episode of the crime drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy captain wearing a colourful costume underneath his uniform is shot and killed in a flower shop, and further investigation reveals he was leading a double life as a masked vigilante. Vance brings in Tony's ex-fiancee, journalist Wendy Miller, who claims the victim had been trying to expose a scandal, and as the team pursues the case, a real-estate scam is uncovered. Guest starring Perrey Reeves (Entourage).(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy reservist is found dead in his home with marks around his neck, and Gibbs and the team discover he was working on a highly classified case for the department of defence. The circumstances surrounding the man's demise lead the investigators to suspect someone was secretly trying to drive him insane, bringing them into contact with Samantha Ryan, the director of a division specialising in psychological warfare. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Chief petty officer Leland Wiley dies of a heart attack just as he is about to reveal information on elusive arms dealer Agah Bayar. The team discovers the cardiac arrest was caused by someone hacking into a computer monitoring Wiley's pacemaker and increasing his heart rate to 400bpm. Meanwhile, a probationary agent tries to impress Gibbs and win a place on his team. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with Tamer Hassan and Matt L Jones.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Confidential files are leaked by an anonymous hacker, who threatens to release more unless the Navy agrees to renew a contract with a struggling shipbuilding firm. The Navy Secretary suspects the company's owner Philip Wickes is to blame, and asks NCIS to join forces with the PsyOps unit in an elaborate ruse to bring the culprit out of hiding. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Cabe is seriously wounded by exploding shrapnel during a desert mission, and the team must freeze his body in order to save his life. Crime drama, starring Robert Patrick.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion accidentally places the US economy in jeopardy by producing counterfeit cash, and Paige must use her mother Veronica's skills as a grifter to save the day.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam meet up with former colleague Mike Renko, who is back in town on the trail of a civilian militia he thinks is responsible for the theft of anti-tank missiles from a Navy base. Their discussion is interrupted by Hetty with footage of a man being strangled at an aquarium - and the victim is identified as a lance corporal who was Renko's inside man at the group.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate the murder of an engineer working on highly sensitive submarine technology, but find the crime scene clean of evidence. Forensic scientist Abby Sciuto travels from the NCIS office in Washington, DC to help out as the case has similarities to a serial killer she has been tracking. Guest starring Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam investigate when former marine Asher Ross is killed by an explosive device hidden in his mobile phone. Because of the sophisticated nature of the bomb, suspicion falls on the members of the ordnance disposal unit Ross was part of in Iraq, but further enquiries reveal that they are being targeted as well.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Navy officer dies following a confrontation involving a dancer and a Sudanese emigre at a strip club. However, when investigations reveal he was a code-breaker involved in naval intelligence, Callen and Sam dig deeper and gradually unearth links to Safar, a Libyan-born terrorist known to recruit on behalf of al-Qaeda. Henri Lubatti guest stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Acting Superintendent Amanda Prosser arrives at Sun Hill, where she makes a good impression on Jo Masters and Phil Hunter. Zain discovers Smithy is sleeping with Louise Larson, and warns him the consequences could be deadly if her husband finds out. June is forced to deal with a date-rape case on her first day back at work, while Yvonne resolves to admit she assaulted a drunk the night before - but reckons without Honey and Tony, who talk her out of it and destroy CCTV evidence of the attack.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As her trial nears, Yvonne learns the drunk she recently assaulted has been called to give evidence against her, and tearfully tells Tony she is sure the prosecution have enough evidence to put her away for a long time. Amber is paired up with Honey and sent to find the man responsible for two date-rape incidents, but puts herself at risk trying to catch the suspect and almost becomes his latest victim. Phil beats up Steve for sleeping with Kate Maltby, while Zain introduces Smithy to an informant who has had two fingers chopped off by Pete Larson, and warns he runs the risk of losing a more intimate part of his anatomy if he's not careful.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jack takes a break from paperwork to help investigate a case alongside CID officers, only to discover his increasingly wayward son Ben may be the mastermind behind a major-league drugs operation. Yvonne's trial gets under way, and Laura Bryant begins work as a PC, while a sceptical Sheelagh kidnaps a youngster allegedly suffering from leukaemia, unwilling to believe there's anything wrong with her.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Yvonne finally learns her fate, and Jack Meadows continues to build a case against son Ben, but discovers to his frustration that he can only muster enough evidence to charge a comparatively insignificant drugs courier. Smithy tells Sheelagh that Gabriel raped Kerry Young, while Gina makes a bad impression on new boss Amanda Prosser by throwing a drink over a copper from Barton Street station.(n)

programme.desc: Henry Crabbe and his wife Margaret become involved with two very different scams in the wine trade - and things fail to go according to plan. Starring Richard Griffiths.(n)

programme.title: Rio Ferdinand: Being Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Former footballer Rio Ferdinand lost his 34-year-old wife Rebecca to cancer in May 2015, and he is still coming to terms with his loss, and the effect it has had upon himself and his three children. Rio meets other families coping with bereavement, and looks at what help is available for parents and children who have experienced loss to move on with their lives.(n)

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert designer Danny Clarke help an elderly gardener in Cumbria, whose pretty plot has been spoiled by building work.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley is on his home patch in north London as he explores the Lea Valley, paying a visit to one of the UK's biggest salad growers to see what impact coronavirus is having. On International Dawn Chorus Day, bird expert Adrian Thomas reveals what to listen out for, shepherdess Hannah Jackson talks about how she's coping with the lockdown, and Adam Henson goes flat out to get his spring barley in.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Rachel de Thame reviews the progress of her new kitchen garden, and begins planting out peas and sowing flower seeds. Nick Bailey shows how to make an easy cold frame for storing precious plants and seedlings, and Joe Swift meets Will Young, whose new-found love of gardening was key to his recovery from mental health problems.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: How planting nettles on the island of Lewis creates the perfect habitat for a rare bird - the corncrake. Plus, Dougie investigates how the milk industry has been hit hard by lockdown, a young crofter sends a dispatch from the Fair Isle, and a look into the long-term impact of flooding.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: In Joppa, George creates a plant support for his sweet peas out of prunings, meanwhile Brian revels in the torrent of tulips at Scone.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The latest two highest-scoring North East chefs cook their six-course menus again to impress judges Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The arbitrators are joined by children's author Jacqueline Wilson, who has written over 100 novels since she started writing books at the age of nine. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare dishes for when the temperature drops, such as oxtail soup with herby dumplings, a chestnut and mushroom pie and a new take on treacle sponge.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a couple's home in Margate. The property was originally a B&B, and the transition to a home has not been entirely successful. It is poorly laid out, has too many rooms, and while big on floor space is lacking natural light. Laura sets out to return the building to its early 20th-century roots, whole Robert comes up with a radically different way of using the living space.(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: From the ancient temples of Angkor Wat in Cambodia to the Shaolin Temple in China, discover what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the opening episode, in Cambodia at Angkor Wat, a man who believes the temples are home to his ancestral spirits risks his life to save them from the jungle, while at the Shaolin Temple in China, a Buddhist warrior monk faces a test that could change the course of his life for ever.(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits some of the great British war films that helped boost the nation's morale in the difficult years that followed the end of the Second World War. Among the silver screen gems highlighted are In Which We Serve, Reach for the Sky, Bridge on the River Kwai and The Dam Busters. The programme includes archive interviews with stars including Noel Coward, Richard Attenborough, David Lean, Alec Guinness, Kenneth More and John Mills, and Sylvia shares her memories of filming her own contribution to the genre, Ice Cold in Alex.(n)

programme.title: The Heroes of Telemark (1965)
programme.desc: German scientists in occupied Norway during the Second World War begin work on the manufacture of vital components for an atomic bomb, prompting the Norwegian resistance to team up with British forces on a campaign of sabotage to halt production. Second World War thriller, starring Kirk Douglas, Richard Harris, Anton Diffring, Roy Dotrice and Ulla Jacobsson.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory in Essex that produces 400,000 cereal bars a day, and follows the production process from the arrival of the ingredients to the eventual dispatch, discovering the importance of getting the perfect balance between honey and glucose. Cherry Healey travels to South Africa to help with the macadamia harvest, where a quarter of the world's supply of these nuts are farmed. Historian Ruth Goodman climbs a mountain in the Lake District to investigate the history of one of the oldest forms of snack bar - the Kendal Mint Cake.(n)

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels through Libya, visiting areas of the country devastated by recent fighting as well as one of the world's best preserved ancient Roman cities. He moves on to Tunisia, where he discovers a mountain fortress and meets a man keeping ancient Berber traditions alive, including making olive oil with the help of camels. Finally, he crosses the sea to Sicily, and discovers how the Mafia are attempting to take advantage of declining population numbers.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, founder of Crafter's Companion, is a new addition to the panel on the business ideas contest, joining returning dragons Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani in putting more would-be entrepreneurs' ideas to the test. Making their pitches this week are the creator of a brand of spiced rum, who gives the studio an exciting new look, a couple who have brought their four-legged friend with them to demonstrate a range of pet food, and the inventor of a special toilet gadget. Evan Davis presents.(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: Sitcom starring American rapper and comedian Lil Dicky as Dave, a neurotic suburbanite who knows he is destined to be one of the world's biggest hip hop stars - but has a harder time convincing everyone else. While hanging around the music studio where his friend works, Dave meets fellow hanger-on GaTa, who attempts to fill him in on the complicated social dynamics of the rap world. He also promises to get him the chance to perform a guest verse for famous rapper YG, who is currently recording in the studio for one of his songs.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: The would-be rapper is finally asked to give his first ever public performance, but the occasion is not exactly what he had always imagined. Elz hesitantly agrees to DJ, while flatmate Mike practices a new skincare routine on Dave.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The housemates awaken to an unexpected crisis with no memory of how it happened, and find themselves put on trial by an international tribunal of vampires. Tilda Swinton heads up a star-studded guest cast, including Dave Bautista (Guardians of the Galaxy), Kristen Schaal (Flight of the Conchords), Danny Trejo (Machete), Wesley Snipes (Blade), Evan Rachel Wood (True Blood), and the stars of the original movie, Taika Waititi, Jemaine Clement and Jonny Brugh, reprising their roles.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: Nadja gives Jenna an introduction to life as a vampire. and trains her how to take her first victim. Nandor applies for American citizenship.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week.(n)

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Istanbul
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles, Edwina Currie, Fatima Whitbread, Mim Shaikh, Amar Latif, Dom Joly and Pauline McLynn spend their last day in Serbia at a monastery hidden in the forests of the Sicevo gorge. Before leaving, Adrian leads the group in a ritual of remembrance with each pilgrim leaving a candle for a loved one. The group travels on to Bulgarian capital Sofia, where Mim and Amar explore its only mosque and Dom, Edwina and Fatima visit the museum of socialist art, a memorial to the nation's communist past. The group reunites and follows the trail towards the Rila Mountains, the highest in the Balkans.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: Documentary revealing what it really takes to deliver Britain's next generation through the eyes of parents and staff at three of Birmingham's busiest maternity hospitals. The programme goes beyond the labour ward to meet parents at every stage of their pregnancy, capturing the work of midwives, doctors and surgeons dedicated to supporting families as they bring new lives into the world. In the first episode, Channelle wants an intervention free birth, Jodie's baby arrives eight weeks early, and there is joy for Ashleigh after a dramatic delivery.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers get to grips with a protective cover for mobile phones, heated mittens and a frying pan able to pour sauces. Presented by Lucy Alexander.(n)

programme.title: Primates
programme.desc: Chris Packham narrates a programme looking at the family of mammals, beginning by revealing the extraordinary strategies monkeys, apes and lemurs must use to survive in the most unexpected places. In South Africa, rarely seen bush babies go in search of food in the lean times of winter, while times are also hard for a troop of bearded capuchins in Brazil's scorched badlands.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, with David Cornock.(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: In the first round of the first heat, hairdresser Oliver, dog walker Katie and photographer Daniela must transform a bedroom each in Regency properties in Cheltenham's Pittville estate. Tom Dyckhoff presents, and is joined by judges Kelly Hoppen and Daniel Hopwood.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The troubleshooter tackles Amelies, a harbour-side seafood restaurant in the picturesque Cornish town of Porthleven. Owner Sam Sheffield Dunstan gave up a high-flying career in London five years ago to fulfil a lifetime ambition of opening an eatery. However, her dream turned into a nightmare as she failed to attract the right head chef, so a desperate Sam turned to Alex to help find the best person for the job. In this edition, Alex puts the first candidates through their paces.(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: The food writer shows people who lack confidence in the kitchen how to pull off an impressive meal for friends and family, demonstrating plenty of deceptively easy recipes and offering invaluable culinary tips. In the first episode, she helps 52-year-old builder Tony - who has never cooked a meal in his life - to master a delicious Mediterranean-inspired menu, including a grilled peach and burrata salad, crispy salmon with polenta chips and grilled asparagus, as well as a showstopping dessert.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott and Len Goodman head to Bradford, where the Strictly Come Dancing judge has his first taste of a Pakistani curry and samples some Latvian cuisine.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Nick Davies scour the crowd at Tilbury Docks in Essex to find objects to take off to auction, while Paul Martin explores the history of the docks.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Farming friends Mark and Sean launch their Herefordshire-inspired crisps at the Droitwich Food Festival, while horticultural pioneer Sophie sells her Juneberry gin.(n)

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian explores Northern Ireland's role in the Second World War, revealing the story of a pilot who became the most successful U-boat hunter of all time. The programme also examines Harland & Wolff's contributions to the development of the famous Churchill tank, and takes an underwater look at the wreck of the U-155. In Donegal, a major excavation reveals the 70-year-old Spitfire flown by American pilot Bud Wolfe, who was forced to bail out just minutes after taking off.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Susan Calman presents as the eight winning chefs from the regional heats battle to get their starter course to the banquet celebrating 150 years of British children's literature, with dishes inspired by everything from Peter Rabbit to Harry Potter. The rivals are cooking to impress Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and guest judge Cressida Cowell, the Children's Laureate and author of the How to Train Your Dragon series.(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Part one of two. The documentary returns to follow the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in tackling the coronavirus pandemic, following staff and patients at London's Royal Free Trust from the first day of the lockdown. The Royal Free Hospital's emergency department is inundated with patients displaying symptoms, among them 88-year-old Peter, who is struggling for breath. He is quickly put on oxygen and moved to what was the coronary care unit, but has now become a ward for covid-positive patients.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended version of the quiz in which Deirdre O'Kane, Richard Osman and David Mitchell join regular panellist Alan Davies to answer host Sandi Toksvig's questions on the theme of 'Occupations and Offices'. During the course of the show, the panellists are set fiendishly difficult questions that lead to points being awarded for the answers the questionmaster considers most interesting, though not necessarily correct.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Stealing Van Gogh
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines the world of high art and organised crime, as he uncovers the true story behind the greatest art heist of the 21st century, when two priceless paintings were stolen from the Van Gogh Museum in Amsterdam in December 2002. Andrew travels across Europe meeting policemen, art experts and prosecutors to try and find out what really happened to the stolen masterpieces.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.sub-title: Scottish Game Fair - Potato Vodka & Game
programme.desc: Two dedicated farming families hope to impress potential buyers at the Scottish Game Fair with farm produced potato vodka and a selection of wild game burgers.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley is on his home patch in north London as he explores the Lea Valley, paying a visit to one of the UK's biggest salad growers to see what impact coronavirus is having. On International Dawn Chorus Day, bird expert Adrian Thomas reveals what to listen out for, shepherdess Hannah Jackson talks about how she's coping with the lockdown, and Adam Henson goes flat out to get his spring barley in.(n)

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels through Libya, visiting areas of the country devastated by recent fighting as well as one of the world's best preserved ancient Roman cities. He moves on to Tunisia, where he discovers a mountain fortress and meets a man keeping ancient Berber traditions alive, including making olive oil with the help of camels. Finally, he crosses the sea to Sicily, and discovers how the Mafia are attempting to take advantage of declining population numbers.(n)

programme.title: Our Finest Hours
programme.desc: As the UK faces its biggest challenge since the Second World War, Sophie Raworth looks at how the nation has responded to adversity in the past and in the present. Using a combination of wartime archive and contemporary news footage, each episode looks at the impact of Covid-19 on families, communities and businesses, and compares how the nation has responded to crisis now and then.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Ochuko Ojiri help two teams at Epsom racecourse in Surrey, while Eric Knowles challenges the crowds to guess the function of an intriguing wooden object.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The makeover show returns, with Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich working for a Bristol couple want a garden that mixes Greek plants with English ones.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Gramophone guru Tim Weeks takes on the repair of an antique wooden phonograph, brought into the Repair Shop by Nicola Dean, whose father was an avid collector. This particular model was his prized possession and the centrepiece of his collection. However, it suffered badly in a collision with a group of overenthusiastic small children, and the distinctive wooden horn has become separated from the main body of the item. Plus, upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary is faced with an unusual challenge when an armchair is brought in, and Steve Fletcher takes on a battered old miner's lamp, which once saved the life of its owner Hubert John.(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: Three contestants are charged with transforming a living room in three Grade II Listed former workers' cottages in the town of Saltaire, West Yorkshire. Retired actor and director Nicholas, marketing assistant James and event stylist Anna each have £1,000, three days and a small team to help deliver their schemes, with judges Kelly Hoppen and Daniel Hopwood deciding which of them are good enough to stay in the competition.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi puts three more head chef candidates through their paces as they compete for a vacancy at Amelies, a seafood restaurant in Porthleven, Cornwall. With one candidate already through to the final, the next three applicants must endure a gruelling interview process. Amiable Irishman Barry hopes to impress with a main course of hare and rainbow pasta, while 20-year-old Chris wonders whether his decision to put a duo of lamb on a seafood menu might prove to be his undoing. Plus, experienced chef Paul refuses to let his work speak for itself as he tries to convince owner Sam he is the right man for the job.(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Bryony Crutcher, 26, admits to passing off shop-bought food as her own creations, but now she's been asked to host her best friend's hen party and needs Lorraine Pascale's help to come up with an impressive meal. The food writer puts together an inspiring canape menu for Bryony to master, including Thai larb gai, bite-sized chocolate Napoleons and delicate coconut meringues filled with ginger cream.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: The Belles of St Trinian's (1954)
programme.desc: The mischievous schoolgirls hope to get rich quick by betting on a racehorse belonging to a classmate's wealthy father. However, they first have to foil a gang of thieves planning to steal the animal and outwit an undercover policewoman sent to infiltrate the school disguised as a teacher. Comedy, starring Alastair Sim, Joyce Grenfell and George Cole.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: The pair reach Leicester, where Len Goodman braves his first ever Indian curry and samples a selection of Greek cuisine. Chef Ainsley Harriott heads to the kitchen to create spiced fish cakes, inspired by the day's adventures in the city.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Bristol Cathedral. Antique experts James Lewis and Philip Serrell find objects to take to auction, including a bronze statue of Buddha.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Cattle farmers and their protegees bring their finest cows to compete at the Driffield Show, hoping to win the much-coveted red rosettes.(n)

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian heads out to sea to hear the story of the Empire Heritage, a cargo ship filled with Sherman tanks sunk off Malin Head during the Battle of the Atlantic in 1944. He gets the opportunity to fly a Second World War aircraft while visiting an RAF flying boat base on Lough Erne, and meets the experts intent on discovering the identities of the remains of soldiers killed during the conflict.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Susan Calman presents the final round of the fish course. Bestselling author Malorie Blackman joins judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver to choose the winner, with entries inspired by authors ranging from Enid Blyton to Phillip Pullman. The cooks also have a special guest in attendance in the shape of author Giles Andreae, one of whose books has been immortalised in edible form.(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The second of two programmes following hospital staff treating Covid-19 cases features patients who are on dialysis or who have recently had a transplant, and as such are among the most vulnerable to the virus. Royal Free London faces a near catastrophic breakdown of oxygen supply, while an infectious diseases consultant starts a trial of an anti-viral drug used in the treatment of Ebola.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Johnny Vegas, Sue Perkins, Mike Tindall, John Barnes and Fleur East play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Peter Sellers - A State of Comic Ecstasy
programme.desc: The comedy pioneer behind the Goon Show, Dr Strangelove and the Pink Panther series is explored in depth in this film surveying his meteoric rise to fame and troubled personal life. The film charts his formative years backstage as part of his parents' itinerant music hall revue group, his years of wartime service in India and Burma, through to his global superstardom. His two surviving children Sarah and Victoria recall the challenges of growing up alongside his mood swings, while his grandson Will explores the troubled legacy his grandfather left behind.(n)

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: A flautist, clairinettist, oboist, recorder player and bassoonist compete for a place in the BBC Young Musician 2020 semi-finals, performing music by Vivaldi, Tchaikovsky, Messiaen and Peter Facer. Anna Lapwood is joined by principal flautist with the Royal Scottish National Orchestra, Katherine Bryan, to review each of the performers.(n)

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.sub-title: Snowdonia - A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: The first of three films exploring life in Britain's national parks. Snowdonia in north Wales is a landscape formed by extinct volcanos, and is home to creatures including otters, hen harriers and peregrine falcons. The programme explores the area over the course of a year through the eyes of people who live and work there, including a warden, a farmer, a climber and a poet. Narrated by Hermione Norris.(n)

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: How Australian cinema celebrates the endurance of outsiders, whether they are newcomers to a strange new land in They're a Weird Mob and Wake in Fright, or locals out of step with the mainstream in Evil Angels, Muriel's Wedding and Priscilla, Queen of the Desert.(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The programme celebrates its 800th episode, with filming taking place under lockdown, as Chris Lintott explores how some astronomers are managing to continue making observations - including controlling telescopes as far away as Hawaii from a laptop in their bedrooms in Belfast.(n)

programme.title: Retreat: Meditations from a Monastery
programme.desc: The second episode follows the a typical day in the life of the 21 monks residing in Pluscarden Abbey, a remote monastery on the edge of the Scottish Highlands in Moray. Given its isolated location, this Abbey is almost entirely self-sufficient, and the monks make their own clothes and grow their own crops, giving them little reason to have contact with the outside world.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: The Ladybird Books Story - The Bugs That Got Britain Reading
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the much-loved children's books, which gained prominence during the 1950s, entertaining and informing youngsters as well as helping generations of pupils to read using their 'Key Words' literacy scheme. Narrated by Miles Jupp.(n)

programme.title: Oceans Apart: Art and the Pacific with James Fox
programme.desc: The art historian traces the effects of the West's contact with the peoples and cultures of the Pacific, beginning with the impact on the Aborigines of Captain Cook's arrival in Australia in 1770. He reveals how the indigenous people were almost destroyed by European colonisation, but held on to their art to survive, flourish and ultimately share their extraordinary culture with the world.(n)

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: A flautist, clairinettist, oboist, recorder player and bassoonist compete for a place in the BBC Young Musician 2020 semi-finals, performing music by Vivaldi, Tchaikovsky, Messiaen and Peter Facer. Anna Lapwood is joined by principal flautist with the Royal Scottish National Orchestra, Katherine Bryan, to review each of the performers.(n)

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.sub-title: Snowdonia - A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: The first of three films exploring life in Britain's national parks. Snowdonia in north Wales is a landscape formed by extinct volcanos, and is home to creatures including otters, hen harriers and peregrine falcons. The programme explores the area over the course of a year through the eyes of people who live and work there, including a warden, a farmer, a climber and a poet. Narrated by Hermione Norris.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene depicting an oval winterscape, featuring a cosy barn and lacy bushes.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Books
programme.desc: The appeal and importance of books, from early texts to paperbacks in the present day. The first episode focuses on the Codex Sinaiticus, the world's oldest surviving bible, which provides an insight into the efforts of early Christians to find a single version of the text that could be accepted by everyone. The programme also looks at another edition of the text produced in Winchester in the 12th century, which reflects a time of struggle between the Church and State for authority.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's History of Modern Britain
programme.desc: The broadcaster charts some of the events that have shaped British society since the end of the Second World War. He begins by exploring Labour's election in 1945, which saw Winston Churchill removed from power, and the nation's struggle to recover from the conflict's crippling effect on industry. Also discussed is the impact of India's independence.(n)

programme.title: Operation Mincemeat
programme.desc: In a documentary based on his book of the same name, Ben Macintyre presents the story of how Britain deceived Hitler into thinking the Allies were planning to invade Greece in 1943, allowing them to land in Sicily - their real target - with minimal resistance. The brainchild of James Bond author Ian Fleming, the deception is thought to have saved thousands of lives.(n)

programme.title: Morocco to Timbuktu: An Arabian Adventure
programme.desc: Writer and explorer Alice Morrison presents the first of two programmes in which she follows the salt roads of North Africa, once the world's richest trading networks, to the fabled sandstone city of Timbuktu. Setting off from Tangier, she learns how the routes were forged by the demand for gold from the mines of sub-Saharan Africa. She then passes through the Islamic city Fes, home to the world's oldest university, catches a train to the great market town of Marrakech and treks across the Atlas Mountains, before visiting the ancient caves of salt, the commodity that gave the roads their name.(n)

programme.title: Elegance and Decadence: The Age of the Regency
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley explores why British construction experienced a boom following the defeat of Napoleon in 1815, and charts how architecture and design were transformed during the period, visiting Brighton Pavilion and Waterloo Bridge. She also tries her hand at waltzing and decorative cake-making, and tells the story of London's Regent Street, which was intended to provide a ceremonial route to the Prince Regent's palace but did not quite live up to expectations.(n)

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore examines the transformation of Vienna into a great cultural capital after the Ottoman threat receded at the end of the 17th century. The city, no longer an outpost defending the West from Islamic invaders, was rebuilt and the Habsburgs vowed their empire and imperial capital would become the most glittering in the world, a magnet for musicians including Mozart, Haydn and Beethoven.(n)

programme.title: Hull's Headscarf Heroes
programme.desc: The story of the women who led a campaign for greater safety at sea after 58 deep-sea fishermen died when Hull trawlers the St Romanus, the Kingston Peridot and the Ross Cleveland sank within three weeks of one another in early 1968.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene depicting an oval winterscape, featuring a cosy barn and lacy bushes.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's History of Modern Britain
programme.desc: The broadcaster charts some of the events that have shaped British society since the end of the Second World War. He begins by exploring Labour's election in 1945, which saw Winston Churchill removed from power, and the nation's struggle to recover from the conflict's crippling effect on industry. Also discussed is the impact of India's independence.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints birch trees, surrounded by a misty background a cheerful blue sky.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Books
programme.desc: A look at the reading material of medieval times, focusing on the shift from manuscripts in the 14th and 15th centuries to the literature that followed. The programme focuses on The Luttrell Psalter, a book of psalms that featured humorous and vivid pictures of rural life and a grotesque demonic world, and Geoffrey Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, which was adopted by William Caxton as the first publication to be created by his printing press in London.(n)

programme.title: Life Drawing Live: Drawing The Nation Together
programme.desc: Josie d'Arby hosts a live broadcast of a life drawing class, allowing viewers to draw along with the students in the socially distanced studio. The models present a variety of poses inspired by classical works of art, while artists Lachlan Goudie, Diana Ali and Nicky Philipps are on hand to share their passion and knowledge.(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC: The Genius of Leonardo da Vinci
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez explores the BBC archives to explore the work of the Renaissance artist, revealing what lies beneath the Mona Lisa and how Da Vinci acquired his anatomical knowledge.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: The Da Vinci Code - The Greatest Story Ever Sold
programme.desc: Timeshift explores the contemporary quest for ancient truths that has arisen out of Dan Brown's best-selling novel challenging the credibility of Christian doctrine. The programme assesses the explosive response to the book from believers and atheists. With contributions by author Richard Leigh and art critic Brian Sewell.(n)

programme.desc: Dani Dyer becomes Rattus' political advisor for tales of parliamentary people and plots.(n)

programme.desc: During the Troubles, Crossmaglen was seen by many as a place apart, but a group of children managed to form one of the most formidable football teams in GAA club history. This documentary follows Crossmaglen Rangers GAC over the course of two seasons as joint managers Oisin McConville and John McEntee - both former All-Ireland winners with the club - attempt to guide the next generation to victory.(n)

programme.title: The Thomas Crown Affair (1999)
programme.desc: A thrill-seeking billionaire develops a taste for cat burglary, and lifts a priceless Monet from a New York gallery in broad daylight. However, he later meets his match in a cunning female insurance investigator and the pair embark on a whirlwind romance - but their conflicting careers threaten their relationship. Crime adventure remake, with Pierce Brosnan, Rene Russo, Denis Leary and Ben Gazzara.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Lockdown: How Business Is Coping - Panorama
programme.desc: Britain's economy has been turned upside down by the coronavirus crisis. Many companies are struggling to stay afloat, while some have found themselves swamped with unprecedented demand. Panorama follows some of the small business owners and key workers struggling through the lockdown - from the courier whose job it is to collect suspected samples of Covid-19, to the funeral director working round the clock to collect and bury the dead.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil puts pressure on Mick and Linda to get the deal done, but is taken aback when he realises Danny has overheard. Ben realises his operation is tomorrow, Kush and Shirley take drastic action to help Jean, and Stuart gets wedding inspiration from an unexpected place.(n)

programme.title: Home Ground
programme.desc: Gavin Andrews travels to the Giant's Causeway to help the National Trust prepare for the return of grazing cattle to the bay after almost half a century. Jo Scott visits Glenview Farm in Co Antrim to learn how horses can help develop life skills, and wildlife expert Conor McKinney investigates the nocturnal actvities of animals in cities and towns.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Connell starts socialising with Marianne in Dublin and is introduced to her group of friends, one of whom is interested in him, while Marianne is still dating Gareth, but her interest in the relationship is starting to fade. Things take a negative turn for the pair when Marianne goes home and argues with her brother Alan and Connell loses the part-time restaurant job that funds his life in the Irish capital. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Dana - The Original Derry Girl
programme.desc: The story of the singer, who won the 1970 Eurovision Song Contest for Ireland with All Kinds of Everything. At a time when the violent conflict of the Troubles was dominating the news, Rosemary Scallon - better known as Dana - became a national treasure overnight while still a teenager. The programme looks at what happened after she won the competition, including her successful pop and TV career in the 70s, her marriage to Newry hotelier Damien Scallon, her move to Alabama, and her stint in Irish politics.(n)

programme.title: Cuckoo
programme.desc: A couple from the Midlands arrive at the airport to meet their daughter, but are horrified to discover she has returned from her gap year with a new husband - a travelling hippie called Cuckoo who is now their son-in-law. First episode of the comedy, starring Greg Davies, Helen Baxendale, Andy Samberg and Tamla Kari.(n)

programme.title: Cuckoo

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before connecting to star guests via the internet. On his 'virtual sofa' in this edition are Sandra Oh, who plays Eve Polastri in the hit spy thriller Killing Eve, and singer-songwriter Niall Horan, who offers a home performance of his single Black and White. Rob Beckett and Romesh Ranganathan discuss their new TV show Rob & Romesh Vs, and Stanley Tucci talks about new book 'Dear NHS: 100 Stories to Say Thank You'. Plus, the Red Chair feature.(n)

programme.desc: Ben prepares for his operation but his mind is elsewhere when Phil shares detail of their upcoming job, leading him to take advantage of Callum to find out what the police might know. Keegan sees a glimmer of hope when Jack tells him a new witness has come forward, Isaac struggles with Sheree's bombshell and Shirley and Kush try to get to the bottom of Jean's explosive mood swings.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: A woman who did not have children until her 40s prepares for the birth of her second son, who was diagnosed with Down's Syndrome early in the early stages of her pregnancy. A couple whose previous child was stillborn at 40 weeks are now preparing for the birth of another baby, but have been tormented with anxiety that the same thing will happen again. A first-time mother fears she will not be a natural parent, a concern that is exacerbated when there is a long delay before she can hold her baby.(n)

programme.title: The A Word
programme.desc: Joe's teacher provides much-needed support as his anxieties over Rebecca's pregnancy grow - but this leads to him becoming fixated on her as his source of security instead of his constantly changing family. Paul is shocked when Mark announces he has applied to join the Army, but is not sure which is more worrying - the hurt he will suffer from being rejected or the danger he will face if accepted. Maurice receives an expensive gift from a member of his hiking group, and fears how Louise will react when she finds out.(n)

programme.title: Call air Cladach Ile (The Loss on Islay's Shore)
programme.desc: The story of two sea tragedies that brought the First World War to the Hebrides. In February 1918, the troopship Tuscania was torpedoed by a German U-boat. More than 200 American soldiers and British crew died as lifeboats were dashed against Islay's cliffs. Eight months later, HMS Otranto collided with another ship in its convoy in a fierce storm, killing nearly 500 men. Just 19 reached Islay's shore alive.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: In England's first National Park, the Peak District, James is joined by restaurateur Tessa Bramley, who serves him a beef fillet alongside vegetables from her own garden. He also takes part in an unusual contest - hen racing - where he challenges a local lad and world champion to a race. After discovering some award-winning gin, James meets a local forager and samples some of his elderflower wine. Finally, he uses some of the foraged elderflowers to make a delicious dessert.(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott's culinary tour reaches Granada in southern Spain, where he learns about the famous Alhambra Palace, enjoys a lesson in flamenco dancing and visits the caves of Sacromante, home to a cooking school where he is taught to make classic tortilla and fried aubergines. Ainsley also samples Spanish hot chocolate - thick, creamy and flavoured with orange - and tries local delicacy piononos, with tour guide Katia. Along the way, he rustles up chicken with saffron potatoes and a classic seafood paella.(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner are joined by Levi Roots,who prepares BBQ salmon, Nadia Sawalha, who makes Mexican chicken, and Shelina Permalloo, who demonstrates a banana and caramel dessert. Plus, recipes for slow-cooked steak chilli, salsa and guacamole, dairy-free coleslaw and a chocolate cake in a mug.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prestigious golf club is the setting for murder when a player is bludgeoned to death on the 13th hole. Barnaby and Jones discover gambling, illegal money-lending and assault are rife among the snobbish members, who are also dismissive of the local villagers and only allow them on the course for a few hours a week. Before long, another body turns up, making the detectives doubly determined to catch this terror of the fairways. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Nicholas Le Prevost, John Standing and real-life golfer Sam Torrance.(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams are once again on hand to judge this week's applicants, and each of them has the chance to put their favourite contestant straight through to the live semi-finals by awarding them the Golden Buzzer.(n)

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hits the road one last time for the concluding installment in this second series of the open-air game show. Once again, Stephen is hitting the streets in a city somewhere in the UK, and setting the members of the public he meets along the way a succession of unusual challenges. Success in these games could give the contestants a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol, to a £1000 cash prize in the big finale game. This week, Stephen's final destination is Belfast.(n)

programme.desc: Ex-convict Russell is far from happy to be isolated with his father Ron, who believes he has brought shame on the family. As Ron succumbs to the virus, Russell must put their differences aside to keep his spirits up. Starring Robert and Tom Glenister.(n)

programme.title: Isolation Stories
programme.desc: Four short dramas depicting life in lockdown. The worst fears of paranoid, self-absorbed hypochondriac Mike have come true. There is an unstoppable virus that affects us all, and he can catch it just by breathing the same air as other people. As he struggles to cope with his fears during the Covid-19 outbreak, he insists on an online session with his old friend and therapist, Rochelle.(n)

programme.title: Isolation Stories

programme.title: Absolutely India: Mancs in Mumbai
programme.desc: Brothers Ryan, Adam and Scott Thomas embark on a journey to trace their family roots in India, joined by their dad Dougie, whose father was born in Mumbai. However, the trio have never visited the country they descended from. In the second episode, Ryan, Adam and Scott attempt to find their inner zen at a huge meditation hall, and Scott gets a fiery fix-up Indian style by a hairdresser who prefers flames to scissors. Later, a special family meeting leads to showman Dougie hitting the stage in Mumbai.(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave wants to learn to play the drums at school, but is given the recorder. With a talent show around the corner, Dave gets practising.(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave and Fuzzypeg the dog get dirty playing at the local dump, so dirty in fact that Fuzzypeg picks up an extremely grumpy flea.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: In England's first National Park, the Peak District, James is joined by restaurateur Tessa Bramley, who serves him a beef fillet alongside vegetables from her own garden. He also takes part in an unusual contest - hen racing - where he challenges a local lad and world champion to a race. After discovering some award-winning gin, James meets a local forager and samples some of his elderflower wine. Finally, he uses some of the foraged elderflowers to make a delicious dessert.(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott's culinary tour reaches Granada in southern Spain, where he learns about the famous Alhambra Palace, enjoys a lesson in flamenco dancing and visits the caves of Sacromante, home to a cooking school where he is taught to make classic tortilla and fried aubergines. Ainsley also samples Spanish hot chocolate - thick, creamy and flavoured with orange - and tries local delicacy piononos, with tour guide Katia. Along the way, he rustles up chicken with saffron potatoes and a classic seafood paella.(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner are joined by Levi Roots,who prepares BBQ salmon, Nadia Sawalha, who makes Mexican chicken, and Shelina Permalloo, who demonstrates a banana and caramel dessert. Plus, recipes for slow-cooked steak chilli, salsa and guacamole, dairy-free coleslaw and a chocolate cake in a mug.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prestigious golf club is the setting for murder when a player is bludgeoned to death on the 13th hole. Barnaby and Jones discover gambling, illegal money-lending and assault are rife among the snobbish members, who are also dismissive of the local villagers and only allow them on the course for a few hours a week. Before long, another body turns up, making the detectives doubly determined to catch this terror of the fairways. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Nicholas Le Prevost, John Standing and real-life golfer Sam Torrance.(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hits the road one last time for the concluding installment in this second series of the open-air game show. Success in these games could give the contestants a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol, to a £1000 cash prize in the big finale game. This week, Stephen's final destination is Belfast.(n)

programme.title: Isolation Stories
programme.desc: Alone, heavily pregnant and fed up with life, Mel faces having to go through the birth of her first child without the married father of her baby, who has chosen to stay with his wife and family. She is without even her own family, who have given her the cold shoulder. Sheridan Smith stars in first of four short dramas depicting life in lockdown during the Sheridan Smith stars in first of four short dramas depicting life in lockdown during the coronavirus pandemic.(n)

programme.title: Isolation Stories
programme.desc: Ex-convict Russell is far from happy to be isolated with his father Ron, who believes he has brought shame on the family. As Ron succumbs to the virus, Russell must put their differences aside to keep his spirits up. Starring Robert and Tom Glenister.(n)

programme.title: Isolation Stories
programme.desc: Four short dramas depicting life in lockdown. The worst fears of paranoid, self-absorbed hypochondriac Mike have come true. There is an unstoppable virus that affects us all, and he can catch it just by breathing the same air as other people. As he struggles to cope with his fears during the Covid-19 outbreak, he insists on an online session with his old friend and therapist, Rochelle.(n)

programme.title: Isolation Stories
programme.desc: A grandad stops off outside his grandkids' window every day to mess about and make them smile. But his son-in-law, still hurting after being left by his wife, does not welcome these daily visits. David Threlfall and Eddie Marsan star in this one-off drama, which also features Eddie's children Blu and Bodhi.(n)

programme.title: Euro 96: The Summer Football Came Home
programme.desc: A look back at the tournament, which was staged in England and saw the hosts enjoy a memorable run to the semi-finals. Highlights on the field included Paul Gascoigne's individual brilliance against Scotland, Stuart Pearce's moment of redemption against Spain and the all-round excellence of England's demolition of the Dutch. With contributions from Terry Venables, Stuart Pearce, David Seaman and Teddy Sheringham.(n)

programme.desc: Yasmeen faces the consequences of her red mist, as the paramedics attend to Geoff. She arrives at the police station to find Imran waiting for her, having taken a call from Ryan, while in the Rovers, the police question the regulars about Geoff and Yasmeen's relationship.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: An exhausted Yasmeen sits in her police cell while the police search No 6 for evidence, unaware that they are being filmed on Geoff's CCTV. programme.desc: Imran watches on as a bewildered and upset Yasmeen is charged, while Paul puts pressure on Billy to chuck Kelly out of the flat after discovering her role in sharing Asha's video. Belle shares a drink at the Woolpack with Jamie, but they're soon joined by a knowing Andrea who tells Jamie she and him need to take a holiday with one another.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will hasn't returned home and Harriet is in the dark over what is going on, while an excited Rishi decides he wants to go camping for his 70th birthday. Priya is disappointed that she has to attend her father's party over spending time with Al, and Belle frets at the thought of Jamie going on holiday with Andrea.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Harriet is angry as Will continues to try to make excuses, while matters between Arthur and Archie get out of hand at Rishi's party, and Vanessa continues with her plans. Wendy is enthusiastic when Victoria leaves her alone with Harry for a brief period, and Dan reveals his fall yesterday has put him back further.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Matron (1972)
programme.desc: A wily thief gathers a band of crooks for a plot to break into a maternity hospital and steal the supply of contraceptive pills. One of the gang gets the lie of the land by disguising himself as a nurse, but the hypochondriac head doctor and formidable matron prove constant obstacles to the heist. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams and Hattie Jacques.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Milton Cross, a feudal estate with a church, farms, houses and acres of land, depend on lord of the manor Edward Milton for their livelihoods. When local woman Sonia Woodley is stabbed to death in the churchyard two years after the death of her husband, bespoke tailor Gerald, Barnaby and Jones investigate, and the village begins to reveal its secrets. Detective drama, guest starring James Wilby, Gwyneth Strong and Karl Davies (formerly Emmerdale's Robert Sugden), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: As Cassie and Sunny continue their investigation into the cold-case murder, the net tightens around the people whose names were found in the victim's diary. Sir Phillip Cross makes a difficult decision to salvage what is left of his reputation while at the same time protecting his family, Father Robert comes clean to the police and Ray returns home to find Lizzie gone. Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Trevor Eve, Bernard Hill, Tom Courtenay, Ruth Sheen and Brian Bovell.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Father Robert's revelations prove to have lasting repercussions, especially for Caroline when she goes into premature labour. Bella and Josh deliberate over the best way to deal with their father, the search for Lizzie continues and Maureen is finally able to lay Jimmy to rest. Conclusion of the crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Bernard Hill, Tamzin Malleson, Zoe Telford, Trevor Eve, Tom Courtenay and Tom Austen.(n)

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: Drama about England and West Ham star Bobby Moore's life with his childhood sweetheart Tina Dean, detailing how the pair rose to the dizzy heights of superstardom. In the opening instalment, the 19-year-old insurance office typist agrees to marry the footballer, little knowing that their marriage will be defined by history. Their lives change markedly when the talented player becomes England captain aged just 23. However, when he is diagnosed with cancer Tina proves Bobby's rock as he fights his way back to fitness in a bid to compete in the World Cup of 1966. Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan star.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The members of an Oxford old boys' cricket team gather for their annual match, unaware that death is waiting at the crease to bowl one of them out for good. Morse sets out to find the culprit - helped as ever by the faithful Lewis, who demonstrates unexpected skill on the pitch. John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Sharon Maughan star in an episode scripted by Oscar-winning director Anthony Minghella.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Du Plessis is hailed as South Africa's answer to Crocodile Dundee as guests arrive at Leopard's Den to watch him in action. However, publicity is the last thing the family wants when pet cheetah Jana runs away from a stunt and has an accident. Meanwhile, a man claiming ownership of a lion demands Danny hand it over - or he will report Jana's injuries to the authorities. Deon Stewardson and Stephen Tompkinson star.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is furious when he finds out what Butch and Sam have been up to. Tina is out walking when she is grabbed by a mystery man. Chris and Kim have another argument about Frank.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave's attempt to make friends with Nick ends in a fight. Dave gives Kim an ultimatum about their future together. Albert returns to the Dingles to face the music with Zak.(n)

programme.desc: An accountant and his family crash into a tractor after being chased down a country lane by another car. Mason is suspicious of them and investigates the incident, discovering they are on the run from a gangster who has arrived in Aidensfield determined to retrieve a set of incriminating documents at any cost. Meanwhile, David buys an old Jeep with a surprising military history. Starring Joe McFadden.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: A Season of Heavenly Gifts - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop's hoard of cash causes trouble for the family when Marcus Cope resolves to stop Charley buying the hop garden at any cost. Montgomery is unhappy at naval college and the Brigadier's solution provides a challenge for Pop.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The housemates are put out when Chrissy's mother arrives for an unscheduled visit. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A flood of forged banknotes leads the police to believe a fugitive gangster has arrived in Aidensfield, but their efforts to flush him out put Dawson in peril. Meanwhile, Miller tries to remain professional while dealing with his relationship problems and Bernie thinks his favourite celebrity has moved in down the road. Gemma Craven guest stars, with Clare Wille, John Duttine, Peter Benson and Joe McFadden.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles prepare to help Albert give himself up. Kim warns Nick not to cross her again. Frank bides his time before letting Kim know he knows about the affair.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rick and Biff's fight spills out on to the street. Frank tells Zoe that the baby isn't his and that Kim has been having an affair. Eric demands his money back from the Dingles.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise makes plans to redecorate. Alec offers Steve £5,000 to leave Vicky alone. Derek worries that everyone is laughing at him behind his back.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bill is nervous about meeting Sally. Audrey tells Alma the gossip about Mike and Deirdre having an affair. Vicky has some harsh words for Alec.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A flood of forged banknotes leads the police to believe a fugitive gangster has arrived in Aidensfield, but their efforts to flush him out put Dawson in peril.
